* Fix RST heading levels and add anchor for Gotchas & FAQ section

The Gotchas & FAQ section was using a numbered heading (5.2.6) with
section-level underlines (dashes), which caused Sphinx to treat it as
a top-level section rather than a subsection. This meant the section
was invisible to RAG retrieval — it was rendered at the wrong nesting
depth and its content was not associated with the "Gotchas" heading in
the chunked documentation.

Fixed by:
- Converting the heading to an unnumbered title with tilde underlines,
  consistent with the surrounding subsection hierarchy
- Adding a .. _gotchas: cross-reference anchor so the section can be
  referenced explicitly by name
- Applying the same heading level correction to all 9 FAQ items within
  the section (items 1–9), which were also using dash underlines at the
  wrong depth

This is significant for AI-assisted modeling: the Gotchas & FAQ section
contains the highest-value practical knowledge in the docs (correct
import paths, create_component usage, StateTracker shape, component
class vs instance, required scenario columns, etc.). With the heading
hierarchy broken, an AI using RAG retrieval would miss this section
entirely when answering questions about common errors. With this fix,
retrieval correctly surfaces the gotchas when users ask about
ImportError, tracker shape mismatches, or component assembly patterns.

fix(state-sync): detect stale sync hash and trigger data reset (#15449)

## Problem

When a node is performing state sync with a sync hash from epoch N, the
network may advance to epoch N+1. Responding nodes replace their state
snapshot early in the new epoch, causing parts for the old sync hash to
become unavailable. The syncing node gets stuck in an infinite retry
loop with no detection or recovery mechanism. This affects both V1 and
V2 sync handlers.

## Solution

A lightweight epoch probe is added in `StateSync::run()`. On each sync
tick, it checks whether `highest_height > sync_hash_block_height +
epoch_length + STALE_SYNC_HASH_THRESHOLD`. If the network has moved past
the sync hash's epoch, the node returns a new
`StateSyncResult::StaleSyncHash` variant, which the handler maps to
`SyncHandlerRequest::EpochSyncDataReset`. The client actor sends
`ShutdownReason::EpochSyncDataReset`, writing a marker file so the
supervisor wipes the DB and restarts fresh on the far-horizon path.

## Detection Threshold

The threshold is set to 100 blocks in production, accounting for epoch
stretching from missing blocks and finality delays. Epochs can extend
beyond `epoch_length` because epoch boundaries require
`last_finalized_height + 3 >= estimated_next_epoch_start`. A 100-block
margin is safe because a false positive would require sustained loss of
finality. Under `test_features` the threshold is lowered to 5 for
testability with `epoch_length=10`.

## Testing

A new testloop test `test_far_horizon_stale_sync_hash_detection`
reproduces the scenario: a fresh node enters StateSync with P2P-only
config (parts always fail), the chain advances past the epoch boundary,
and the node detects the stale hash and triggers shutdown. The test is
gated on `test_features` since it depends on the lowered threshold.

[MrBot] Add coding guidelines from zrpc close race PR review (#362)

* Add coding guidelines from zrpc close race PR review

Learnings extracted from PR #15148895 (zrpc) review feedback:

- Variable naming: never use abbreviated names (hn, cio, tw -> host_name, client_io_config, etc.)
- Assert NOT_NULL after every create call in test code
- Initialize volatile_atomic variables with interlocked_exchange, not direct assignment
- Use InterlockedHL_SetAndWakeAll instead of separate set + wake_by_address_all
- Use enums with INTERLOCKED_DEFINE_VOLATILE_STATE_ENUM for state tracking, not magic integers
- Use ASSERT_FAIL in unexpected error callbacks instead of (void)context

Exclude gitignored files from Lambda layer and source code bundles (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: exclude gitignored files from lambda layer and code bundles

- Add gitignore_exclude_patterns() utility to bundler.py that reads the
  root .gitignore and returns all non-comment, non-blank patterns
- DepsBundler.__init__ now stores patterns; try_bundle passes them to
  shutil.copytree(ignore=...) so __pycache__, *.pyc, .venv/, etc. are
  excluded when copying workspace packages into the layer

  ignore_mode=IgnoreMode.GIT to Code.from_asset for the lambda handler,
  keeping gitignored artefacts out of the deployment zip

* fix: apply ignore callback at top level of workspace src copy

Replace the manual entry-by-entry loop with a single shutil.copytree call
on src_dir directly, so the ignore callback (built from .gitignore patterns)
is applied at every level including the top level of src/. The old loop
only passed ignore= when recursing into sub-directories, meaning top-level
__pycache__/, *.pyc, and similar entries would still be copied into the layer.
